name: August Corn Chowder
recipeIngredient: 2 Corn ears, kernels removed (reserve cobs halved crosswise)
4 Red-skinned new potatoes, unpeeled, cut into 1/4 inch dice
3 Cucumbers, peeled, seeded and diced into 1/4 inch pieces
1 small Red bell pepper, cored, seeded and diced into 1/4 inch pieces
Coarsely ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Defat the chicken broth completely for best results.
2. Add fresh dill stems; simmer 10 minutes. Discard cobs and stems.
3. Stir in potatoes until tender (8 minutes). Add corn kernels (1 minute).
4. Strain soup through a fine mesh sieve into a bowl, reserving vegetables and broth.
5. Pulse yogurt with 2/3 of diced cucumbers in food processor; pulse to combine without fully blending.
6. Pour reserved broth through feed tube and pulse until combined.
7. Transfer mixture to serving bowls. Stir in remaining vegetables, chopped dill, and black pepper.
8. Refrigerate for up to 2 hours before serving chilled.
Per 1-cup serving: 212 calories, 2 grams fat, 5 milligrams cholesterol.
